---
title: "Michael P. Kerr"
description-meta: "Michael P. Kerr — applied math and geography at UGA. Research in topological data analysis, scientific machine learning, and computational geography."
toc: false
about:
    template: trestles
    image: profile.jpg
    links:
        -   icon: file-earmark-pdf
            text: CV
            href: cv.pdf
        -   icon: envelope
            text: michael.kerr@uga.edu
            href: mailto:michael.kerr@uga.edu
        -   icon: github
            text: Github
            href: https://github.com/mpk15956
        -   icon: linkedin
            text: LinkedIn
            href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/mpk15956
---

I am a computational geographer integrating high-performance computing, machine learning, and spatial statistics to address pressing problems. My research transforms complex, multi-dimensional data into interpretable models, emphasizing spatial optimization and algorithmic equity to reveal meaningful, scale-invariant patterns. As I transition to graduate research, I am focused on deploying computer vision and the neural rendering models (NeRFs) for real-time edge hardware. Exploring methods for learning from differential equations and tensor methods, I aim to develop physics-based models that bridge advanced mathematics with actionable strategies for environmental resilience and social equity.

## Research Interests
* **Computer Vision** & Edge Computing 
* **Spatial Optimization** & Algorithmic Fairness
* **Topological Data Analysis (TDA)** & Spatial Statistics
* **Scientific Machine Learning (SciML)** & Tensor Decomposition

## Education
**University of Georgia**, Athens, GA  
*B.S. in Geography & Applied Mathematics* (Expected July 2026)
